title: cracca bracteolata
intro: Cracca bracteolata, also known as the stick-leaved broom or broom leaved milkwort, is a perennial flowering plant native to Europe and Asia. It has been used for centuries in traditional medicine, valued for its anti-inflammatory and diuretic properties. The plant is said to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving digestive issues, and treating urinary problems. Herbal preparations of Cracca bracteolata include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
